title: Enzo and the Missing Moon Rocks
synopsis: When the class pet hamster's moon rock treats mysteriously disappear, young scientist Enzo teams up with his diverse classmates to solve the case using observation and teamwork.

pages.text: Enzo loved science class. He wore his favorite lab goggles every day. Today, Mrs. Chen had a special surprise. "Class, meet Luna!" she said. Luna was a fluffy white hamster. She lived in a big glass tank with tubes and wheels. "Luna eats special moon rock treats," Mrs. Chen explained. "They help her teeth stay strong." The treats looked like tiny gray pebbles. They sparkled in the light.

illustration.description: A diverse classroom scene with Enzo, a curious boy with dark curly hair wearing bright blue lab goggles, looking excitedly at a white hamster in a colorful habitat. Mrs. Chen, an Asian teacher with a warm smile, stands nearby. The classroom has science posters and bright decorations.
illustration.alt: Enzo and his classmates meeting Luna the hamster

pages.text: "Can I feed Luna?" asked Enzo. "Me too! Me too!" said his friend Amara. She had beautiful braids with colorful beads. Mrs. Chen smiled. "You can all take turns." She showed them the special treat jar. It was full of sparkly moon rocks. "Only give Luna two treats at a time," she said. Enzo counted them. There were twenty treats in the jar. He loved counting and measuring things.

illustration.description: Enzo and Amara, a girl with intricate braids and colorful beads, looking at a clear jar filled with sparkly gray pebbles. Mrs. Chen holds the jar while other diverse students gather around. The scene is bright and cheerful with warm classroom lighting.
illustration.alt: Students learning about Luna's special treats

pages.text: The next morning, Enzo rushed to see Luna. Something was wrong! The treat jar was empty. "Mrs. Chen!" Enzo called. "Luna's moon rocks are gone!" Mrs. Chen looked surprised. "That's strange," she said. "I filled the jar yesterday." Enzo put on his thinking face. This was a mystery! He needed to find clues. "Don't worry, Luna," he whispered to the hamster. "I'll solve this case!"

illustration.description: Enzo looking concerned and determined, holding an empty glass jar while Luna the hamster looks up at him from her habitat. The morning sunlight streams through classroom windows, creating a mystery atmosphere with dramatic lighting.
illustration.alt: Enzo discovering the missing moon rock treats

pages.text: Enzo started his investigation. He looked for clues around Luna's tank. "What are you doing?" asked Marcus, his friend with freckles and red hair. "The moon rocks are missing," Enzo explained. "Someone took them!" Marcus gasped. "That's terrible! Luna needs her treats." They searched together. Enzo found tiny crumbs near the water fountain. "Aha!" he said. "These look like moon rock crumbs!"

illustration.description: Enzo crouched down like a detective with a magnifying glass, examining tiny crumbs on the floor near a colorful water fountain. Marcus, a boy with red hair and freckles, kneels beside him. Both boys have expressions of concentration and excitement.
illustration.alt: Enzo and Marcus finding clues near the water fountain

pages.text: More friends joined the search. Sofia had long black hair and always asked good questions. "Who was here first this morning?" she wondered. "I was!" said Enzo. "But the treats were already gone." Jamal, who loved solving puzzles, had an idea. "Maybe someone came after school yesterday?" They made a list of suspects. The janitor? Another teacher? A hungry student? This mystery was getting interesting!

illustration.description: A diverse group of children huddled together making a detective plan. Sofia has long black hair, Jamal is a Black boy with a thoughtful expression, and they're all writing on a colorful notepad. The classroom background shows educational posters and Luna watching from her habitat.
illustration.alt: The detective team making a list of suspects

pages.text: "Wait!" said Amara. "Look at Luna's cheeks!" Everyone crowded around the tank. Luna's cheeks were very puffy. They bulged out like balloons. "Oh my!" laughed Mrs. Chen, who had been watching them. "I think we found our thief!" Enzo was confused. "What do you mean?" Mrs. Chen explained, "Hamsters store food in their cheek pouches. Luna must have eaten all her treats at once!"

illustration.description: Luna the hamster with comically puffy cheeks stuffed full of food, looking adorable and guilty. The children's faces show surprise and delight as they peer into the habitat. Mrs. Chen is laughing in the background with her hand covering her smile.
illustration.alt: Luna the hamster with very puffy cheeks full of treats

pages.text: "But how did Luna get the treats?" Enzo wondered. He was still thinking like a scientist. Mrs. Chen smiled. "Let's investigate together." She showed them Luna's habitat. The clever hamster had learned to open the treat jar! Luna had pushed it over and rolled it to her food dish. "She's very smart," said Mrs. Chen. "Too smart for her own good!" Everyone laughed. Even Luna seemed to wiggle her whiskers.

illustration.description: Mrs. Chen pointing to Luna's habitat setup while the children watch in amazement. Luna sits proudly next to an overturned treat jar and her food dish. The scene shows the path Luna took with tiny paw prints, creating a playful crime scene recreation.
illustration.alt: Mrs. Chen explaining how Luna solved the treat jar puzzle

pages.text: "Mystery solved!" announced Enzo proudly. He felt like a real detective. "Luna was the treat thief all along!" The class cheered. Mrs. Chen gave Luna fresh water to help wash down all those treats. "From now on, we'll keep the jar in a safe place," she said. Enzo learned something important that day. Sometimes the best mysteries have the silliest answers. And the best detectives work as a team!

illustration.description: The whole diverse class celebrating around Luna's habitat, with Enzo taking a bow like a successful detective. Everyone is smiling and clapping. Luna is drinking water from her bottle with slightly less puffy cheeks. The classroom is filled with joy and bright, warm colors.
illustration.alt: The class celebrating the solved mystery

pages.text: That afternoon, Enzo drew pictures of the whole adventure in his science journal. He drew Luna with puffy cheeks and the empty treat jar. He wrote: "Today I learned that hamsters store food in their cheeks. I also learned that good detectives ask questions and work with friends." When his mom picked him up, Enzo couldn't wait to tell her about the Great Moon Rock Mystery. "Tomorrow," he said, "I'll solve another case!"

illustration.description: Enzo at his desk drawing in a colorful science journal, with detailed sketches of Luna and the investigation visible on the pages. His mom, a warm woman with kind eyes, looks over his shoulder admiringly. The scene has golden afternoon light streaming through windows.
illustration.alt: Enzo documenting his detective adventure in his science journal

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was special about Luna's treats?
questions.explanation: Luna ate special moon rock treats that looked like sparkly gray pebbles and helped keep her teeth strong.

questions.question: Who really took Luna's treats?
questions.explanation: Luna had eaten all the treats herself and stored them in her cheek pouches, making her cheeks very puffy.

questions.question: How did Enzo find clues?
choices: He looked around and asked questions
questions.explanation: Enzo searched for physical clues like crumbs and worked with his friends to ask good questions about the mystery.

questions.question: What do hamsters use their cheek pouches for?
questions.explanation: Hamsters have special cheek pouches where they store food, which made Luna's cheeks look very puffy and round.

questions.question: How did Luna get the treats out of the jar?
choices: She pushed the jar over and rolled it
questions.explanation: Luna was clever enough to push over the treat jar and roll it to her food dish so she could eat all the treats.

pages.title: Fill in the Mystery Words
pages.instructions: Help Enzo solve the case by filling in the missing words!
pages.text: Enzo put on his _____ goggles and looked for clues. He found tiny _____ near the water fountain. When they looked at Luna, her _____ were very puffy because she had stored all the treats inside them!

pages.title: Detective Questions
pages.instructions: Complete these questions that a good detective would ask:
pages.text: Sofia asked, '_____ was here first this morning?' Jamal wondered, 'Maybe someone came after _____ yesterday?' These were great _____ that helped solve the mystery!
